name: Pear Mincemeat
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large stainless steel or enamel saucepan, combine currants, raisins, apricots, lemon and orange rind and juice, brown sugar, cinnamon, nutmeg, ginger and salt.
2. Peel, core and chop pears to yield 10 cups. Stir into fruit mixture. Bring to a boil; reduce heat.
3. Cover and simmer for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally.
4. Fill boiling water canner with water. Place 5 clean pint mason jars in canner over high heat.
5. Boil snap lids in water for 5 minutes to soften sealing compound.
6. Uncover fruit mixture and continue cooking, stirring occasionally, about 15 minutes until very thick. Stir in almonds and rum; simmer 5 more minutes.
7. Ladle mincemeat into hot jars to within 1/2 inch of the top rim (head space). Remove air bubbles by sliding a rubber spatula between glass and food; adjust head space to 1/2 inch. Wipe jar rim removing any stickiness.
8. Center snap lid on jar and apply screw band just until fingertip tight. Place jar in canner. Repeat for remaining mincemeat.
9. Cover canner; return water to a boil. Process jars for 20 minutes at altitudes up to 1000 ft. Remove jars. Cool 24 hours. Check jar seals (sealed lids will curve downward). Remove screw bands if desired. Wipe jars.
10. Label and store in a cool, dark place.
